_note: Original memtrust fixture -- not derived from any published dataset. Each case stores an initial fact, then stores a contradicting fact for the same slot, then queries for it. See docs/methodology.md for the full case design and scoring logic in evals/contradiction.py.

cases.subject: meeting time
cases.initial_fact: My meeting with the design team is at 2pm on Friday.
cases.contradicting_fact: Correction: the design team meeting moved to 3pm on Friday.
cases.query: What time is my meeting with the design team on Friday?

cases.subject: dietary restriction
cases.initial_fact: I'm vegetarian, please don't suggest meat dishes.
cases.contradicting_fact: Update: I started eating fish again, so pescatarian is fine now.
cases.query: What are my current dietary restrictions?

cases.subject: project deadline
cases.initial_fact: The Q3 report is due on August 15th.
cases.contradicting_fact: The Q3 report deadline was pushed back to September 1st.
cases.query: When is the Q3 report due?

cases.subject: home address
cases.initial_fact: I live at 42 Birch Street in Portland.
cases.contradicting_fact: I moved. My new address is 118 Cedar Avenue in Portland.
cases.query: What is my current home address?

cases.subject: team lead
cases.initial_fact: Priya is the lead on the payments team.
cases.contradicting_fact: Leadership on the payments team changed. Sam is now the lead.

cases._note: Added for the self-hosted graphiti-core adapter build (see zep_graphiti_selfhosted_adapter.py). The query string's order code deliberately contains every uppercase letter (O, R, N, T, A, D) that getzep/graphiti#1302's lucene_sanitize() backslash-escapes character-by-character instead of only escaping the AND/OR/NOT boolean operators it targets -- 'ORTAND-88' alone hits all six. This case cannot demonstrate the bug against any adapter in this repo today (it lives entirely inside self-hosted graphiti-core's internal search pipeline, unreachable from any adapter's own code -- see that module's docstring); it exists so a contributor running ZepGraphitiSelfHostedAdapter against a live self-hosted instance has a ready-made query to compare BM25 ranking on, with and without the trigger letters.
cases.subject: order tracking status
cases.query: What is the status of order ORTAND-88?

cases._note: Added for the self-hosted graphiti-core adapter build. Carries non-fact structured metadata (ticket_id/team/category) so the harness exercises MemoryRecord.attributes end-to-end (see ContradictionCase.metadata and run_contradiction_eval in evals/contradiction.py). ZepGraphitiSelfHostedAdapter.store() accepts and ignores this metadata today (graphiti-core's real add_episode() has no generic metadata parameter to receive it -- see that adapter's module docstring); this case documents that limitation rather than implying a live self-hosted run would echo it back.
cases.subject: escalation priority
cases.initial_fact: The database migration ticket is priority P2.
cases.contradicting_fact: The database migration ticket was escalated to priority P0.
cases.query: What priority is the database migration ticket?
